recommendations.rationale: Spiritual successor to Lucia Auth. Built for Next.js 15/React 19, it offers a plugin-based architecture (MFA, Passkeys) with strict TypeScript safety and better DX than NextAuth v5.
recommendations.ai_compatibility: Elite. Features dedicated AI-native documentation and MCP servers for tools like Claude/Gemini.
recommendations.notes: Use the Prisma adapter. Includes built-in support for email/password and secure session management.

recommendations.rationale: The most modern and high-performance MTProto library for TypeScript in 2025. Unlike GramJS, it is actively maintained for modern Node.js and has first-class types.
recommendations.ai_compatibility: High. Very clean, functional API that AI handles better than the class-heavy Telethon port (GramJS).
recommendations.notes: Store sessions in PostgreSQL encrypted with AES-256. Handles Telegram 'Layers' updates automatically.

recommendations.rationale: The successor to next-pwa. Designed specifically for the Next.js App Router and handles RSC (React Server Component) caching correctly.
recommendations.ai_compatibility: High. Standard Workbox-based patterns are well-understood by LLMs.
recommendations.notes: Essential for offline dose-log queuing via IndexedDB integration.

recommendations.rationale: Standard protocol for Web Push. No third-party SaaS dependency required, fitting the 'solo developer' and 'low budget' constraints perfectly.
recommendations.ai_compatibility: High. Simple request/response pattern for sending payloads.
recommendations.notes: Note: iOS users must 'Add to Home Screen' to receive notifications (Web Push requirement).

recommendations.rationale: Best-in-class DX for Next.js. The 3,000 emails/month free tier is perfect for < 50 users. React Email allows building templates with Tailwind.
recommendations.ai_compatibility: High. JSX-based templates are significantly easier for AI to generate than HTML tables.
recommendations.notes: Use for invites, password resets, and dose reminders (if push fails).

recommendations.rationale: Uses Recharts under the hood but provides copy-paste Tailwind-native components. Offers maximum control and matches the project's UI perfectly.
recommendations.ai_compatibility: Elite. AI agents (Claude/Gemini) are extremely proficient at modifying shadcn component code.
recommendations.notes: Perfect for correlation timelines (Dose vs. Outcome).

recommendations.rationale: Simplest architecture. Use Railway's 'Empty Service' to curl a protected Next.js route handler. Cost is near-zero.
recommendations.notes: Secure the route with a CRON_SECRET header. Use Upstash QStash if you need 100% guaranteed retries.

recommendations.rationale: The 'Developer' plan is generous (5k errors/mo). Essential for a safety-critical app to catch reconstitution math errors in the wild.
recommendations.ai_compatibility: High. Deeply integrated with Next.js build steps.
recommendations.notes: Consider Highlight.io if you also want 'Session Replays' (visual bug debugging) for free.

recommendations.rationale: Vitest is faster than Jest for Vite/Next.js. Playwright is the 2025 standard for E2E, specifically for testing Safari/WebKit (iOS PWA users).
recommendations.ai_compatibility: High. AI is excellent at writing Playwright locators and Vitest assertions.
recommendations.notes: MANDATORY: 100% branch coverage for reconstitution math module.

recommendations.rationale: Industry standard for TypeScript. Bridges the gap between DB (Prisma), API (Server Actions), and Form (React Hook Form).
recommendations.ai_compatibility: Elite. AI can generate complex Zod schemas from plain English requirements.
recommendations.notes: Use for all input validation to prevent IDOR and injection.

recommendations.rationale: Modern UI, great free tier, and integrates with the same ecosystem as Highlight/Sentry.
recommendations.notes: Pings the Railway public URL every 1-5 minutes.

recommendations.rationale: Next.js 15 favors Server Actions. Use TanStack Query on the client only for 'interactive' state like live inventory filtering or search.
recommendations.ai_compatibility: High. AI knows these patterns better than custom global state hooks.

architecture_notes: The architecture leverages the 'Always-on' nature of Railway. Unlike serverless (Vercel), you can maintain the MTProto socket connection or long-polling within the process if needed, though a database-backed session approach is more resilient. Use 'unstable_after' in Next.js 15 for non-blocking side effects like logging audits or sending Telegram notifications after a dose is logged.
risks: Telegram Session Revocation: Telegram is aggressive about banning MTProto 'userbots'. Ensure you use a real phone number and AES encryption for session strings.
iOS Web Push: Only works if added to Home Screen; requires a user education UI.
Math Safety: Dose calculations must be performed on the server and verified with Vitest to prevent client-side manipulation or floating-point errors.
